regional_requirements.description: Canadian federal and provincial laser safety regulations

federal_legislation.purpose: Regulate sale and importation of radiation emitting devices including lasers

provincial_requirements.description: Provincial Occupational Health and Safety acts apply to laser workplaces
provincial_requirements.contact: Contact provincial regulators for show permits
provincial_requirements.alignment: Canadian standards align closely with ANSI Z136 series

entertainment_specific.indoor_shows: No authorization required but MPE limits apply
entertainment_specific.form_requirement: Notice of Proposal to Conduct Outdoor Laser Operation(s)
entertainment_specific.form_similarity: Similar to US FAA Advisory Circular 70-1
entertainment_specific.submission: Submit to appropriate Civil Aviation Regional Office
entertainment_specific.laser_pointer_laws: Strict regulations in Montreal, Toronto, Vancouver since June 2018

key_definitions.radiation: Energy in the form of electromagnetic waves or acoustical waves
key_definitions.radiation_emitting_device: Any device capable of producing and emitting radiation, plus components and accessories
key_definitions.manufacturer: Person engaged in manufacturing, modifying, or assembling radiation emitting devices
key_definitions.distributor: Person engaged in selling or leasing radiation emitting devices

sale_lease_import.prohibition: No sale, lease, or import of non-compliant devices
conditions: Must not create risk of genetic or personal injury, impairment of health, or death
Must perform according to claimed characteristics

deception.prohibition: False, misleading, or deceptive labeling, packaging, or advertising

notification.requirement: Manufacturers/importers must notify Minister of non-compliance or defects

inspectors.certificate: Must produce certificate of designation when required

powers.entry: May enter premises at reasonable time
powers.examination: Examine and remove devices for further examination
powers.records: Examine and copy books, reports, test data, records

obstruction.prohibition: No obstruction, hindrance, or false statements to inspectors

seizure.authority: Inspector may seize devices if contravention believed

analysts.analysis: Inspectors may submit devices for analysis
analysts.certificates: Analysts may issue certificates or reports of results

consent.provision: Minister may dispose of device with owner's consent

forfeiture.unclaimed_devices: Forfeited to Crown if unclaimed within 60 days
forfeiture.proceedings: Forfeiture does not apply if proceedings instituted

unlawful_imports.inspector_discretion: May allow owner to remove, consent to forfeiture, or take measures

governor_in_council_powers: Prescribe classes of radiation emitting devices
Prescribe standards for design, construction, and functioning
Prescribe label information and manner of display
Respect inspector and analyst powers, duties, functions
Generally carry out purposes and provisions

interim_orders.authority: Minister may make interim orders for significant risk
interim_orders.duration: 14 days unless approved by Governor in Council
interim_orders.max_duration: One year or shorter specified period
interim_orders.tabling: Must be tabled in Parliament within 15 days

other_provisions.summary_conviction: Fine up to $1,000 or imprisonment up to 6 months or both
other_provisions.indictment: Fine up to $3,000 or imprisonment up to 1 year or both

employee_agent.provision: Employer liable for employee/agent offences unless due diligence exercised

forfeiture_on_conviction.provision: Device may be forfeited on conviction

requirements: Ensure devices do not create health/safety risks
Notify Minister of non-compliance or defects forthwith
documentation: Shipping bills and bills of lading

requirements: Ensure imported devices comply with Canadian standards
Notify Minister of non-compliance or defects
Cooperate with inspection and seizure procedures
unlawful_imports: Inspector may require removal from Canada

outdoor_shows.form: Notice of Proposal to Conduct Outdoor Laser Operation(s)

when_inspector_arrives: Request to see certificate of designation

page_analyses.content: Act may be cited as Radiation Emitting Devices Act
Definitions of analyst, distributor, inspector, label, lease, manufacturer
Definitions continued; devices not covered by Act
exclusions: Motor vehicles under Motor Vehicle Safety Act

page_analyses.content: Section 4-5: Prohibitions on sale, lease, import, and false labeling
Section 6: Notification of non-compliance or defects
Sections 7-8: Inspector designation, certificates, powers
Section 8 continued: Warrants for dwelling-houses, assistance, detention
Sections 9-10: Obstruction prohibition, seizure authority
Section 11: Analyst designation and analysis authority
page_analyses.key_provision: Analysts may issue certificates of analysis

page_analyses.content: Section 12: Disposition of devices with owner consent
page_analyses.provision: Minister may dispose of device with owner consent

page_analyses.content: Sections 12.1-12.2: Forfeiture provisions, unlawful import procedures
Sections 12.3-12.4: Cost recovery, certificates of default
Section 13: Governor in Council regulation-making powers
page_analyses.scope: Broad authority for standards, labelling, records, enforcement

page_analyses.content: Section 13 continued: Specific regulation powers listed
Section 13(2)-(3): Publication requirements, exceptions
page_analyses.process: Regulations published in Canada Gazette with opportunity for representations

page_analyses.content: Section 13.1: Interim order authority for significant risks
page_analyses.duration: 14 days to 1 year depending on approval

page_analyses.content: Section 13.1 continued: Tabling requirements
page_analyses.requirement: Must table in Parliament within 15 days

page_analyses.content: Section 14: Penalties for contraventions
Sections 15-16: Employee/agent liability, forfeiture on conviction
page_analyses.defence: Due diligence defence for employer liability

page_analyses.content: Date modified: 2020-06-26

key_personnel.inspectors: Designated under Section 7, have entry and examination powers
key_personnel.analysts: Designated under Section 11, conduct technical analysis
key_personnel.manufacturers: Must comply with standards and notify of defects
key_personnel.distributors: Subject to inspection and record requirements

related_legislation: Provincial Occupational Health and Safety Acts
